nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 几内亚湾
nodes.summary: 几内亚湾是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括非洲长尾鹰、灰頸岩鶥、Blackcap illadopsis等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 比奥科岛
nodes.summary: 比奥科岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括Cameroon olive pigeon、Blue-throated roller、Blue-moustached bee-eater等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 低繁殖率
nodes.summary: 繁殖周期长且单次成功育雏率有限。

nodes.name: 灰頸岩鶥
nodes.summary: 灰頸岩鶥（Grey-necked rockfowl，学名 Picathartes oreas），在图谱中标记为近危（NT）物种，主要分布于比奥科岛、几内亚湾，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、森林砍伐、低繁殖率。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|alt=Mount Cameroon emerges through the clouds in the background, while the foreground depicts dense rainforest|The forests around Mount Cameroon are a nesting area The grey-necked rockfowl is found in West Africa from southeast Nigeria to southwest Gabon. In Nigeria, it is only found in the nation's southeastern corner near the coast and the Cameroon border. The species is widespread in southwestern Cameroon, and this country is considered to be the species' stronghold. It is found throughout Equatorial Guinea and into southwestern Gabon. Additionally, the species resides in the southwestern forests of the island of Bioko in the Gulf of Guinea. There is only one record of this species from the Republic of the Congo, though it is suspected that the rockfowl may have an undiscovered population in this country. The grey-necked rockfowl's total range covers approximately . The grey-necked rockfowl prefers rugged terrain in these forests covered in large boulders, caves, and gorges. Additionally, it often found near inselbergs and a source of water, either a river or a forest pool. The understory of its forests has sparse undergrowth or open spaces but is covered in mosses, ferns, lianas, and epiphytes. In southwestern Bioko it is found in low forests that receive nearly of rain a year. Bioko's habitat also has dense undergrowth and vertical gorges near a caldera. The rockfowl's habitat is normally found between above sea level, though it is lower in elevation on Bioko. This species is non-migratory, and at one site in Cameroon the birds remained within of their nesting site throughout the year. It is capable of living near human activity, and one breeding site in Cameroon was located within of a maize plot. This and other recent observations suggest that the rockfowl has greater tolerance for degraded habitat than previously thought

links.label: 分布于

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: This species is classified as vulnerable as its dwindling and fragmented populations are threatened by habitat destruction

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|right|alt=A farmer in Cameroon makes his way up a hill, surrounded by his large-leafed crop.|A farmer's field on Mount Cameroon The grey-necked rockfowl is considered to be vulnerable due to habitat destruction, its isolated populations, collection of adults, predation, and a low breeding success rate. Its habitat is being destroyed to create large agricultural fields and cocoa plantations, as well as for logging and slash-and-burn agriculture. Due to the highly specialized requirements for its habitat, its population is very fragmented, and the species is believed to be naturally rare.{{cite journal | last = Harter| first = B

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|right|alt=A farmer in Cameroon makes his way up a hill, surrounded by his large-leafed crop.|A farmer's field on Mount Cameroon The grey-necked rockfowl is considered to be vulnerable due to habitat destruction, its isolated populations, collection of adults, predation, and a low breeding success rate. Its habitat is being destroyed to create large agricultural fields and cocoa plantations, as well as for logging and slash-and-burn agriculture. Due to the highly specialized requirements for its habitat, its population is very fragmented, and the species is believed to be naturally rare.{{cite journal | last = Harter| first = B
